title: verbascum neglectum
intro: Verbascum neglectum, also known as the flax-leaved mullein, is a biennial plant belonging to the figwort family, native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are derived from its leaves and flowers. The health benefits of Verbascum neglectum include soothing coughs, reducing fever, and alleviating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural remedy enthusiasts.
